commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8aa756ffa18896de9908bc3691b12ee1e69f7065)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds local-mode handling for TUI `/status` and `/compact` plus a regression test asserting those two commands do not call `sendChat`.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +14. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not a live run: current main routes slash submissions to `handleCommand`, sends unhandled names through `sendMessage`, and local embedded `sendChat` invokes `agentCommandFromIngress`. That establishes the reported fallthrough path for local mode.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human/contributor follow-up because the fix is incomplete for sibling advertised commands and the external PR proof gate cannot be satisfied by ClawSweeper automation.

target.title: fix(tui): block unsupported local slash commands
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep local embedded TUI `/status` and `/compact` out of the model/chat send path.
- Show an explicit local-mode system response instead of adding a user prompt.
- Preserve gateway-mode `/status` forwarding through the shared command path.

Fixes #71592

## Proof

```
git config user.name && git config user.email
```

```
Alix-007
li.long15@xydigit.com
```

```
node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module <<'EOF_PROOF'
import { createCommandHandlers } from './src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts';

const sent = [];
const users = [];
const systems = [];
const state = {
  currentAgentId: 'main',
  currentSessionKey: 'agent:main:main',
  currentSessionId: null,
  activeChatRunId: null,
  pendingChatRunId: null,
  pendingOptimisticUserMessage: false,
  activityStatus: 'idle',
  isConnected: true,
  sessionInfo: {},
  agents: [],
};

const { handleCommand } = createCommandHandlers({
  client: {
    sendChat: async (payload) => {
      sent.push(payload);
      return { runId: 'r1' };
    },
    getGatewayStatus: async () => ({}),
    listModels: async () => [],
    listSessions: async () => ({ sessions: [] }),
    patchSession: async () => ({}),
    resetSession: async () => ({ ok: true }),
  },
  chatLog: {
    addSystem: (text) => systems.push(text),
    addUser: (text) => users.push(text),
    reserveAssistantSlot: () => {},
    clearTools: () => {},
    setToolsExpanded: () => {},
  },
  tui: { requestRender: () => {} },
  opts: { local: true },
  state,
  deliverDefault: true,
  openOverlay: () => {},
  closeOverlay: () => {},
  refreshSessionInfo: async () => {},
  loadHistory: async () => {},
  setSession: async () => {},
  setEmptySession: async () => {},
  refreshAgents: async () => {},
  abortActive: async () => {},
  setActivityStatus: (text) => {
    state.activityStatus = text;
  },
  formatSessionKey: (key) => key,
  applySessionInfoFromPatch: () => {},
  applySessionMutationResult: () => false,
  requestExit: () => {},
  runAuthFlow: async () => ({ exitCode: 0, signal: null }),
});

await handleCommand('/status');
await handleCommand('/compact now');
console.log(JSON.stringify({ users, sentMessages: sent.map((payload) => payload.message), systems }, null, 2));
EOF_PROOF
```

```
{
  "users": [],
  "sentMessages": [],
  "systems": [
    "local embedded TUI mode does not support /status; message not sent",
    "local embedded TUI mode does not support /compact; message not sent"
  ]
}
```

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts
```

```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
   Duration  29.53s
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 40.71s
```

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/commands.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts
```

```
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```
pnpm exec oxlint src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts
```

```
(no output; exit 0)
```

```
git diff --check
```

```
(no output; exit 0)
```

## Notes
- I did not run an interactive full-screen TUI session; the proof calls the production command handler directly and the focused TUI command tests cover the routing behavior.
